summary: This chunk contains extensive Latin consecration prayers for the Munich Handbook of necromancy (CLM 849), focusing on divine invocations to empower ritual texts and spirit-binding experiments. It lists numerous sacred names (e.g., On, Alpha et O, Tetragramaton, Emanuel) and emphasizes their power over elemental forces (earth, air, fire, water) and spirits (both celestial and infernal). Ritual instructions include nine-day book consecration, use of holy water, the sign of the cross, Confiteor recitation, and psalms. Biblical figures like Moses, Noah, Daniel, and Jonah are invoked for their exorcistic authority.
key_passages: Hoc factum per ix dies, erit liber consecratus, et quocienscumque aliud consecrare volueris, incipias primam oracionem legere, scilicet Deus invisibilis...
...per sanctissima nomina tua - On, Alpha et O, El, Eloye, On, Otheon, Stimlamathon,-Ezelphares, Tetragramaton...
Quo audito a mare regreditur, aer conmutatur,- vel N. concitatur,- terra tremit, ignis extingwitur, omnisque exercitus terrenus et infernalis tremet...
...per sanctissimum nomen tuum quod scribitur 4or litteris, scilicet Ioth, Varo, Hee, Teth, quo audito mare regreditur...
Hoc factum per ix dies, erit liber consecratus...
